behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is primarily multi-module handoff (git-derived branch facts + age threshold + PR-state join), which unit tests can miss. Behavior text does not require live network/subprocess verification or externally visible round-trip output, so System is not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Run detector end-to-end with a temp local repo fixture and injected PR-state provider; verify classification only when all three predicates are true (ahead-of-main, older-than-threshold, no-open-PR).

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This is about real session bootstrap behavior and an externally visible warning signal. To avoid false confidence, verify emission through actual startup path/output surface (SYSTEM TRIAD #3 side-effect visibility). No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real session bootstrap entrypoint in a controlled stale-branch scenario and assert the warning appears in emitted output/log stream with branch name and action context.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a local deterministic mapping function with no I/O, no infra, no model call, and no multi-step pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over branch-state inputs to assert exact label outputs (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) and no nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live repo + live PR state and rendered externally visible output. That triggers real external contract/round-trip verification (SYSTEM TRIAD #1/#3).
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in an environment wired to real git repo state and real PR source, then assert rendered output shows `clean` vs `STALE` in the corresponding branch scenarios.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is correct orchestration of branch-state and PR-status interpretation across modules. Behavior text does not require live external contract checks; can be validated with integrated fixtures/stubs for PR states.
behaviors.test_description: Run staleness evaluator with branch scenarios linked to `open` and `merged` PR statuses via integrated data path; assert classifier never returns `stale-no-pr` for those statuses.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Rebase/squash correctness depends on real git history transformations and commit graph behavior that mocks often hide. Verifying true post-rewrite state requires real VCS/OS/subprocess interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Create real git scenarios with rebase and squash-merge flows, run detector after history rewrite, and assert branch is no longer classified as unPRed stale when equivalent work is integrated.
